Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Agenda item 126: Proposal from the Moderate Party (M) – Ensure a democratic handling of the citizen initiative regarding Rörberg Airport. Reference number: 26KS284 Decision The Municipal Board decides to reject the proposal.

Description of the matter Pekka Seitola (M) submits the following proposal: Proposal – Ensure a democratic handling of the citizen initiative regarding Rörberg Airport.

The citizen initiative to preserve Gävle Airport, Rörberg, has engaged thousands of Gävle residents. A total of over 10,000 signatures were collected in support of a referendum. After the municipality's verification, 7,875 signatures were approved, while the threshold for ten percent of eligible voters was 8,253 signatures. Subsequently, approximately 670 additional signatures were submitted, which the municipality chose not to count.

The Municipal Council in Gävle decided to reject the citizen initiative, citing that the number of approved signatures fell below ten percent of eligible voters, and that the municipality did not consider itself obligated to count the additional signatures submitted. On May 8, 2026, the Administrative Court of Appeal in Falun issued a ruling in the case, overturning the Municipal Council's decision to reject the citizen initiative "Preserving Gävle Airport." The Administrative Court of Appeal stated that the municipality had no legal basis to disregard additional signatures when calculating the total. The court wrote, among other things, that the municipality, through its actions, had imposed additional formal requirements for citizen initiatives beyond what is stated in the law, which risks undermining the purpose of the regulations to strengthen local democracy. This is a very serious development. The issue is not only about Rörberg Airport, but also about the right of Gävle residents to have their democratic initiatives reviewed in a correct, legally sound, and lawful manner. Page 9 (120)

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 In light of the above, we propose that the municipal board recommend to the municipal council that they decide: that the municipality of Gävle should promptly ensure that all submitted and supplementary signatures on the popular initiative "Preserving Gävle Airport" are counted and verified in accordance with current legislation.

Motions Pekka Seitola (M) proposes that the initiative be approved. Richard Carlsson (KD) and Jan Myléus (KD) support this motion. The chairperson proposes that the initiative be rejected.

Decision The chairperson puts the two motions to a vote and finds that the municipal board rejects the initiative.

Dissenting Opinions Paula Larsson (M), Pekka Seitola (M), Eva Marja Andersson (M), Richard Carlsson (SD), Elizabeth Puhls (SD), and Jan Myléus (KD) dissent, stating that they believe the initiative should be approved.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Agenda item 127: Initiative from the Moderate Party (M) - Review The management and governance of the Care and Welfare Board. Reference number: 26KS283 Decision The Municipal Board decides: to reject the initiative. Description of the matter Pekka Seitola (M) submits the following initiative: Initiative - Review of the management and governance of the Care and Welfare Board. As early as 2023, serious irregularities were discovered at the Fleminggatan nursing home, where staff subjected residents to physical and psychological abuse, which had been ongoing for a long time. The municipality investigated and confirmed a number of very serious shortcomings. At approximately the same time, serious and long-standing irregularities were also uncovered at the Solberga nursing home. The assessment was that there were insufficient conditions for healthcare to provide individual residents with good and safe care at Solberga. There was a lack of adequate management and governance of the Care and Welfare Board's operations, where shortcomings were not prevented, but only addressed reactively after individuals had suffered harm. Now, three years later, the Care and Welfare Board's management and governance are still not good enough. Now, major problems have been discovered at the Forellplan nursing home in Sätra. Residents have, among other things, not been allowed to shower, have been put to bed with their clothes on, and have been allowed to sleep through meals. There are also serious conflicts between staff members. It appears that shortcomings are still being addressed too late, in a process where residents have been affected and a nursing home is in crisis. According to Chapter 6, Section 1 of the Municipal Act (2017:725), the Municipal Board has a duty, among other things, to oversee all boards. During this term of office, we have seen recurring and serious shortcomings within the Care and Welfare Board, where the health and safety of the elderly are at risk in an unacceptable way. Based on this, we, the Moderates, believe that the Municipal Board needs to exercise its supervisory duty and review the Care and Welfare Board's management and operational governance. Page 11 (120)

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Regarding the above, we propose that the Municipal Board decide: to review the management and governance of the Care and Welfare Committee.

Motions Pekka Seitola (M) proposes that the motion be approved. Richard Carlsson (SD) supports this proposal. The chairperson proposes that the motion be rejected.

Decision-making process The chairperson presents the two proposals and decides that the Municipal Board rejects the motion.

Dissenting opinions Paula Larsson (M), Pekka Seitola (M), Eva Marja Andersson (M), Richard Carlsson (SD), and Elizabeth Puhls (SD) dissent, stating that they would have approved the motion. Documents related to the matter:

  • Motion from the Moderate Party (M) - Review the management and governance of the Care and Welfare Committee, case number 26KS283-1.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Agenda item: 129 E-petition #416232 - Candlelight vigil along Gavleån River on January 27th to commemorate the victims of the Holocaust. Reference number: 25KS536 To be sent to: Unit for Social Sustainability, Governance and Support Decision The Municipal Board, following the proposal of the chairperson, decides:

  1. That the e-petition is considered answered.
  2. That the administration is tasked with investigating how the municipality can further develop its efforts to commemorate Holocaust Remembrance Day, including the intentions of the e-petition regarding a candlelight vigil.

Description of the matter The Municipal Board has received an e-petition proposing that the municipality annually commemorate Holocaust Remembrance Day on January 27th, a national day of remembrance, by lighting candles along Gavleån River and adjacent streets, in a manner similar to the "Å-draget" event. This is to honor the memory of those who were murdered during the Holocaust, as well as those who opposed it, and to bring the city's residents together for community and learning. Gävle municipality usually commemorates the day with an activity; the date is included in the libraries' activity calendar, and suggestions for lesson materials are sent to schools. In 2023, the municipality participated in a local Holocaust Remembrance Week. The program was coordinated by the Church of Sweden and was a collaboration between various organizations. The "Å-draget" event, which the e-petition proposes to emulate for Holocaust Remembrance Day, is a well-known and visible public celebration held on the first Saturday of September, and this year marks its 25th anniversary. Page 15 (120)

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 "Å-draget" is an event consisting of many activities in various locations throughout the city, and the lighting of torches along the Gavleån river is a symbol of the event. The torches, their placement, lighting, and collection are handled by an external company that the municipality pays for. The current price for this service is approximately 80,000 Swedish krona. The total budget for "Å-draget" was 200,000 Swedish krona in 2025, and this year the budget has been increased to 350,000 Swedish krona.

If Gävle municipality were to light a large number of torches in prominent locations in the city center on Holocaust Remembrance Day, the public would need to understand the reason for this. Therefore, torches on the streets would require a significant public information campaign, and for the municipality to effectively communicate this information, it needs to carefully consider the message it wants to convey. What do we want people to remember in connection with this? What do we want residents to learn? The message needs to be clear and widely accepted by the general public. In recent years, the discussion about the Holocaust has become increasingly politicized, which requires special consideration. Unattended open flames always pose a safety risk, and due to the increased antisemitism in recent years, there could be a particular risk of counter-actions and vandalism, especially on Holocaust Remembrance Day. Therefore, the torches would need to be monitored. However, Gävle municipality encourages external organizations to take the initiative to organize events on Holocaust Remembrance Day and to seek collaboration with Gävle municipality in this regard.

If the municipal board were to proceed with the proposal in the e-petition, the issue needs to be investigated further. For example, it needs to be determined what message the municipality wants to send, a risk and consequence analysis needs to be conducted, and it needs to be clear which department is responsible for commemorating Holocaust Remembrance Day and from which budget the costs would be covered.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08

Motions The chairperson proposes: To respond to the online petition. To instruct the administration to investigate how the municipality can improve its efforts to commemorate the Holocaust Remembrance Day, including the intentions of the online petition regarding candles. Marita Johansson (V), Richard Carlsson (SD), and Paula Larsson (M) propose that the online petition be approved.

Decision-making process The chairperson puts forward the motion to respond to the online petition against the motion to approve the online petition. Dissenting opinions Marita Johansson (V), Richard Carlsson (SD), Elizabeth Puhls (SD), Eva-Marja Andersson (M), Pekka Seitola (M), and Paula Larsson (M) express their dissent in favor of approving the online petition.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Agenda item 130: Environmental Report for Gävle Municipality for 2025 Case number: 26KS5 To be sent to: All committees and boards. Decision The Municipal Board, following the proposal of the chairperson, proposes that the Municipal Council decide to: approve the environmental report for Gävle Municipality for the year 2025. Description of the matter Gävle Municipality's environmental strategy is reviewed annually through an Environmental Report. The report is used to visualize progress in environmental and climate work and serves as a basis for continued planning and implementation of the strategy. The Environmental Report includes a review of 34 goals and 60 indicators, with the circular economy plan integrated into the review. Statistics for the indicators have been collected from designated contact persons within the municipal organization and from national monitoring systems. During the year, activities within the municipal organization have reported on completed measures. The goal analyses have been prepared in collaboration with relevant representatives within the municipal organization. On December 15, 2025, the Municipal Council decided to update the environmental strategy. This report reviews the environmental strategy before the update. The Environmental Report compares the 2025 goal status with the year 2020, when the strategy was revised. The purpose is to show how progress has been made over time. The development since 2020 shows that environmental work is moving in the right direction. Fewer "red" goals and an increase in both "green" and "yellow" goals indicate that the measures taken are having an effect. Compared to 2020, the percentage of "green" goals has increased from 9 percent to 18 percent, while the percentage of "yellow" goals has increased from 47 percent to 59 percent, and the percentage of "red" goals has decreased from 44 percent to 23 percent. Page 18 (120)

Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Continued challenges exist in reaching our target levels, particularly in the areas of:

  • Reducing emissions related to transportation
  • Water quality
  • Contaminated sites
  • Increasing local food production.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Agenda item: 132. First half-year report for 2026 with annual forecast for the Municipal Board. Reference number: 26KS21 To be sent to: The decision will be sent to all departments within the sector, as well as to the Finance Department and Sector Director Katarina Stistrup.

Decision: The Municipal Board decides, in accordance with the chairperson's proposal, to approve the activity report for the first half-year and its appendices.

Description of the matter: The Finance Department of the Governance and Support Sector has prepared the activity report for the first half-year of 2026, including the annual forecast for the Municipal Board.

The interim report details the board's progress towards its goals, financial results, and annual forecast, as well as investments, follow-up of development activities, realized gains/losses, and sales/purchases of land.

The interim result is 51.8 million SEK, compared to a budgeted amount of 45.6 million SEK. The difference is due to a larger surplus in the activities funded by municipal grants, amounting to 11.4 million SEK (primarily due to personnel costs and consulting fees), and lower revenues and higher costs within the development activities. The activities funded by external contracts (IT) have a result of 6.7 million SEK.

The annual forecast for the entire year is 88.3 million SEK, compared to a budgeted amount of 130.3 million SEK. The large negative difference is primarily due to the development activities, while the activities funded by municipal grants show a surplus of 10.6 million SEK, and the activities funded by external contracts show a surplus of 2.6 million SEK.

Case Description The Governance and Support sector has compiled the first half-year report for 2026 for the municipality and its municipal companies. The report presents the financial results and forecasts of the committees, the municipality, and the wholly-owned companies, as well as investments and forecasts for the entire year. Performance monitoring is carried out at the committee and company level. A consolidated report and a description of activities and goal achievement at the municipal and municipal group level will be reported in the second half-year report.

For the period January-April 2026, Gävle municipality shows a financial result that is affected by increased costs in areas such as social care, LSS (a Swedish law concerning support and service for people with disabilities), placements, and winter road maintenance. At the same time, higher tax revenues, net financial income, and certain operational surpluses contribute positively. The full-year forecast for the municipality is SEK 194.6 million, which represents a positive result but lower than the budget. Several committees are forecasting deficits at the end of the year. The largest negative deviations are expected to occur within:

  • The Municipal Planning Committee (-33.7 million SEK)
  • The Social Committee (-30.0 million SEK)

Page 25 (120)

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08

  • Labor Market and Functional Impairment Committee (-20.0 million SEK)
  • Education Committee (-10.0 million SEK)

The population of Gävle has increased slightly during the first few months of the year, primarily due to positive net immigration. The number of children and young people is decreasing, while the number of elderly people is increasing, particularly in the group over 80 years old.

The target for a strong financial position is projected to be 53.4 percent, which exceeds the target of 50 percent. The municipality's balance sheet result is projected to be 187.3 million SEK, and the municipality has no previous deficits to recover. Overall, Gävle municipality, despite some projected deficits in operations, demonstrates a strong financial position.

The municipal group projects a positive result for the entire year, and the municipal companies consistently report positive profit forecasts.

The investment rate remains high, even though several major projects have been postponed.

Description of the matter Municipal Council's framework decision This decision is valid on the condition that the Municipal Council confirms the borrowing limit of 15,280 million SEK in conjunction with the approval of the budget for the year 2026 and the outlook for 2027-2029, as detailed in Appendix 2, the Municipal Council's budget plan. Since the outstanding debt is repaid over several years, the borrowing decision must include both the existing debt, the borrowing required to finance the year's expenses, and a certain liquidity reserve. In order to limit the interest costs of the municipal companies, the Municipal Council, in the Municipal Plan 2026, decides to authorize the municipality to borrow and provide credit, as well as to provide guarantees to the companies, within a limit of 15,280 million SEK (14,070 million SEK). The total borrowing volume, including guarantees for the municipality of Gävle and the municipal companies, amounts to 15,780 million SEK (14,570 million SEK) according to the supplementary budget for the year 2026. The Municipal Board is authorized to take out temporary liquidity loans of 1,000 million SEK as a precautionary measure. Financing of the municipal group The reason why the municipality has chosen to assume responsibility for the overall financing of the municipal group is as follows, according to the financial policy adopted by the Municipal Council: • The municipal group constitutes a financial unit and is considered as such in the credit market. Page 36 (120)

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 • The consolidated creditworthiness of the municipal group should be utilized to achieve the lowest possible financing costs for the entities within the group. The municipality has a higher credit rating than the municipal companies and should therefore act as the borrower in the financial market. • The owner, Gävle municipality, bears a hundred percent responsibility for its wholly-owned subsidiaries. • Gävle municipality can never allow any company within the municipal group to be forcibly liquidated or declared bankrupt. Fund Management The rules for the placement of the municipality's and foundation funds are regulated by the financial policy for the Gävle municipal group (KF 29/3 2021) and the investment policy for foundations (KF 24/9 2007). Delegation Given the way the credit market functions with telephone trading, the Municipal Board's decisions during procurement processes cannot be awaited. Therefore, the Municipal Board has previously delegated the decisions regarding borrowing. These borrowing decisions have subsequently been reported to the Municipal Board on an ongoing basis. The Municipal Board is proposed to apply the same procedure during the year 2026.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 § 143 Statement on the audit report - Review of the systematic occupational health and safety work at Gävle Port. Reference number: 26KS186 To be sent to: Municipal Audit Committee SG-Finance Decision The Municipal Board, following the proposal of the chairperson, decides to adopt the statement below.

Description of the matter Azets Revision & Rådgivning AB, on behalf of the lay auditors of Gävle municipality, has conducted two reviews of the systematic occupational health and safety work. One review concerns Gävle Energi AB, Gävle Energi Elnät AB, and Gävle Kraftvärme AB, and the other concerns Gävle Hamn AB.

Both reviews, and the supplementary assignment (a review of the management of governing documents within the group of companies) carried out by Azets Revision & Rådgivning AB, show that the companies have handled the municipal council's governing documents in different ways. The reviews have also shown that the process for establishing the municipal council's governing documents has not been entirely clear to the companies.

Based on the observations and assessments made in the reviews, the municipal auditors have made the following recommendation to the Municipal Board: • Clarify the process for decision-making regarding governing documents that should also apply to the municipal companies. The auditors request a statement from the Municipal Board with comments on the recommendation by no later than June 26, 2026.

The Municipal Board's statement: Page 56 (120)

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 The Municipal Board shares the auditor's assessment that the process for decision-making regarding governing documents that also apply to municipal companies should be clarified.

The auditor's recommendation is considered to be partially addressed, as the Municipal Board, during the most recent adoption of governing documents, specifically proposed that the Municipal Council urge the companies to, in turn, make a decision to adopt the document as their own. "The Municipal Board decides to propose that the Municipal Council adopt the insurance policy and urge wholly and partially owned subsidiaries and municipal associations to adopt this insurance policy." (Insurance policy 26KS40). This wording will now be included as a standard clause in decisions regarding other governing documents.

To strengthen the process, the "Procedure Manual" (21KS380) will be supplemented with clarifications for case handlers, specifically instructing them to consider when the municipality's companies are covered by the decision. This is to ensure correct decision formulation and that the relevant companies are informed of the decision.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Agenda item: 149 Archipelago Study, Gävle Municipality, 2026 Reference number: 26KS269 To be sent to: Business Development Unit Decision The Municipal Board resolves to:

  • Initiate an archipelago study for the municipality of Gävle.
  • Assign the study to Gästriklands Besöksnäringsförening (Gästrikland Tourism Association).
  • Allocate 150,000 SEK from the Business Development Unit's budget to finance the study.
  • Present the findings of the study to the Municipal Board in October.

Background The municipality of Gävle aims to enhance opportunities for both residents and visitors to experience an accessible, attractive, and sustainable coastal and archipelago environment. This includes exploring the potential of archipelago transportation to facilitate access for both residents and visitors, and to contribute to a wider range of attractions in our coastal and archipelago areas. The study will also focus on identifying the needs and opportunities for the tourism industry, identifying potential development areas, and analyzing how accessibility considerations align with development potential.

The assignment is given to Gästriklands Besöksnäringsförening, which represents the tourism industry in Gästrikland. The association works to ensure that the tourism industry has the best possible conditions for continued growth and an increased share of the Swedish tourism market. There is some uncertainty regarding whether this assignment requires a formal procurement process. Therefore, the association should investigate this matter and is recommended to seek support from the municipality of Gävle.

Funding for the study will be provided from the Business Development Unit's budget.

Proposals The chairperson proposes:

  • That an archipelago study be conducted for the municipality of Gävle.
  • That the study be carried out by Gästriklands Besöksnäringsförening (Gästrikland Tourism Association).
  • That the study be financed with 150,000 SEK from the business development unit's budget.
  • That the study be presented to the municipal board in October.

One of the main changes in the new fee schedule is that the use of outdoor seating areas is proposed to be permitted year-round. A new category for other uses is introduced, with the purpose of being used in cases where a purpose cannot be classified into another category. A fee for evening and night-time use of market squares is introduced, and a so-called "Stockholm arrangement" is also introduced, which allows for differentiated fees within the building purpose category.

Another change is that several categories are removed or merged. Previously, there were many different sales categories; several of these are combined in order to simplify fee collection and provide better predictability for permit holders. Market trading locations are simplified to refer to the number of days. Page 78 (120)

The purpose is to clarify that it is the purpose of the permit that determines whether it is subject to a fee, not the specific area being used. Another change is the introduction of lower fees. This primarily applies to outdoor seating areas, events, and advertising signs. "Urban gardening" is proposed to be made exempt from fees. The municipality's own fee for processing permit applications for the use of public spaces is being removed. According to Chapter 2, Section 5 of the Municipal Act, a municipality can only charge fees for services and amenities that it is obligated to provide if it is required by law or other regulations. The Act on the Municipality's Right to Charge Fees for Certain Uses of Public Spaces, etc., states that the municipality can charge for the permit holder's use of the public space, not for the municipality's processing of applications as defined in Chapter 3, Section 1 of the Public Order Act. The zoning classification within the fee schedule for the use of public spaces has also been revised. Background On December 15th, the Municipal Council decided to support the POG Committee's report with proposed changes, which are to take effect from December 1st, 2027. Subsequently, the Municipal Council also approved new regulations based on these changes.

The committee also assigned the Municipal Director to formulate a proposal for a new name for the "Sector for Governance and Support."

The assignment lacked a clear purpose, other than that some employees had expressed that a name change would be beneficial, and that there was a potential risk that citizens might confuse the sector with the new Support and Care Committee. The estimated costs of changing various administrative systems alone are calculated to be between 0.5 and 1 million Swedish krona, in addition to further costs for things like signage replacements. Given the unclear purpose and uncertain benefits in relation to the costs incurred, the administration recommends that the name "Sector for Governance and Support," with the abbreviation SG, should remain unchanged.

Municipal Board Meeting date: 2026-06-08 Agenda item 166: Disposal of physical originals related to accounting records Reference number: 26KS36 To be sent to: Jessica Kallysson, Economic Services, Governance and Support John Frölander, Information Management Unit, Governance and Support Decision The Municipal Board decides, in accordance with the chair's proposal, to adopt the proposed decision regarding the disposal of physical originals for accounting records that have been digitized. Background With the entry into force of the government's proposal 2023/24:78, the requirements for the preservation of physical originals for accounting records have been eased. Specifically, physically received or created originals, such as incoming invoices and receipts, no longer need to be retained, provided that they have been correctly digitized. This does not affect the retention period for accounting records as stipulated by municipal accounting law, but only the conditions for how the documents should be stored and in what formats. Since there are no longer any legal requirements to retain physical originals in their original format, it is possible to dispose of them. This still requires a disposal decision from the relevant departments. A disposal decision for physical originals related to digitized accounting records would significantly reduce the need for physical storage space for the administration's financial functions, as well as for rented storage space used by the scanning service, which currently stores the majority of the municipality's invoices in storage until the retention period for the documents expires, at which point the disposal is carried out. In order for the decisions to be implemented effectively in practice by the central financial function within Governance and Support, it is necessary for all departments to adopt the decision. Key discussion points revolved around how the municipality's funds should be allocated and how land should be utilized for new projects.
lattlast:
The municipal council in Gävle has reviewed several important issues for the city's future. This includes examining financial reports and determining the amount of funding to be allocated in the budget for the coming years, 2027 to 2030. They have also discussed the governance and oversight of the municipality's various companies.

Regarding land and construction, politicians have discussed agreements for new areas, such as Skogmur and Kungsbäck. This involves determining who can use municipal land and what regulations apply to construction in those areas. Discussions have also taken place regarding wind power and how to address the energy company Vattenfall's need for land in Överhärde.

Several issues related to the city's life and environment have also been addressed. This includes everything from supporting events like Gävle Pride and Brynäs IF, to discussing nature reserves and environmental initiatives. They have also discussed how to manage working conditions and safety in places like Gävle port and Gävle Energy.

Finally, the meeting focused on allocating funds to various purposes. This could include support for youth and sports, as well as funding for the maintenance of places like Stortorget and Kasthallen. Discussions have also taken place regarding the costs associated with using public spaces.
